rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAI YUET CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the statutory fee based on the Inventory and Appraisal alone, without detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales, would have been no more than $34,897.01. As there were no objections to the $45,000 attorney statutory fee request, based on the final amount received from the Hong Kong estate, the Court will overlook the incorrect "Corrected" Inventory and Appraisal and improper calculation of the statutory fee. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHNNIE FORD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for lack of response to tentative ruling posted on 12/22/21 for the hearing on 1/11/22. Courtesy copies and a proposed order still have not been received by the assigned Examiner as of 1/21/22. No proof of service has been filed yet. The petition does not appear to have been supplemented yet to include a prayer. If this is meant to be the two-year accounting ordered on 6/23/21, no bank statement has been filed to verify the cash on hand. Furthermore, the I&As on file were all appraised by the administrator, when it appears that not all assets were cash (e.g., personal property and a Fidelity retirement account). The Final I&A includes a "Penalty Amount Owed by Tonia Richardson" of $47,494.15, but that was an amount ordered during administration, not an asset on the date of Decedent's death. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CATHERINE WHITCOMB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Petition To Invalid Trsut Amendment
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioners Peter J. Whitcomb, Jr., Peter Alexander Whitcomb, and Bryanna Nicole Whitcomb's Motion to Amend Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment. The unopposed motion is GRANTED. The court may grant leave to amend the pleadings at any stage of the action. Motions for leave to amend the pleadings are directed to the sound discretion of the judge. "The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading." CCP Section 473(a)(1); see CCP Section 576. The court's discretion will usually be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6C.3d 920, 939; Mabie v. Hyatt (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 581, 596 (citing text); Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428. Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint "at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial," absent prejudice to the adverse party. Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761 (internal quotes omitted). Petitioners Peter J. Whitcomb, Jr., Peter Alexander Whitcomb, and Bryanna Nicole Whitcomb shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. Although there has been a waiver of account the final report still needs to be filed. Where there has been a waiver of accounts or a deferral of court assessment fees, the conservator of the estate must file a final report stating the current circumstances of the estate re: the need for a final account or the possibility of payment of the assessment fee. When the Court learns that a conservatee has died and no final account or report has been filed, the Court will set a status date for filing of the final account or report. [LRSF 14.48 I. 5)]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOANNE ANDERSON REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 25, 2020 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Finding And Correcting Scrivners Error In The Trust And For Confirmation Of Assets In The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. The 1994 Grant Deed shows the real property held as "Queen Ester Anderson, a Married Woman, as her sole and separate property and Joanne Anderson, an un married woman". Although there is an affidavit Death of Joint Tenant filed in 2007 the real property does not appear it was held in joint tenancy according to the 1994 Deed and it appears it was held as tenants in common. Please explain the legal basis/legal authority for ownership of the property. It appears if there are other issue/heirs of Queen Ester Anderson they may be entitled to an interest in the real property and need to receive 30 days' notice of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 02/07/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF REBECCA MATTESON PRUSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please address Probate Code 7052 and file evidence that shows San Francisco is the proper jurisdiction and venue. 2) If a citizen of a foreign country dies without leaving a will or leaves a will without naming an executor, or if it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code 8113] 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 5) Please have Petitioner file a waiver of bond. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/7/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
